rationale: The citing paper’s core problem is social desirability bias in marketing surveys and it explicitly contrasts randomized response with SDR-scale adjustment (including an impression-management measure), which aligns closely with the cited paper’s contribution on conceptualizing/measuring SDR (ERT/MRT) and guidance on interpreting SDR correlations. Given this direct construct and measurement overlap, the citation is plausibly a substantive theory/measurement reference rather than an incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
